theme: budgeted tastings to map a phase change
title: Budgeted Tastings: Mapping a Hidden Phase Plateau
strategies: do-nothing baseline: run zero experiments, predict the energy-conservation-only weighted average everywhere (reproduces the evaluator's zero-experiment reference)

composition-space-filling sweep at a fixed B-fraction, then sort readings by the linear predictor and 1-D interpolate/extrapolate a single smooth curve; ignores the public transition-window hint and the B-fraction dependence of the plateau's width, so it is blind on the narrow transition band

anchor-plus-window-focused design: a few broad anchor experiments plus the bulk of the budget swept finely inside the public transition-window hint at a moderately large B-fraction, using repeated flat readings to read off the transition temperature directly and bisecting toward its edges to pin the latent-heat scale
regime-classifying prediction from the fitted (transition temperature, latent-heat scale) pair, applying the matching closed form per test mix instead of one global curve
